era_context: The Neo-Assyrian Empire under Ashurnasirpal II continues its expansionist phase. The Levantine states remain under Assyrian pressure. Egypt is fragmented. Greece is in the Geometric period. This year falls within the period of Assyrian hegemony that will define the Near East for the next century.

events.title: Ashurnasirpal II's continued Levantine campaigns
events.description: Ashurnasirpal II continues his military campaigns in the Levant, further consolidating Assyrian control and establishing tributary relationships with local city-states. The campaigns are documented in royal inscriptions that record military victories and the imposition of Assyrian administrative control.
events.certainty_note: Ashurnasirpal II's reign is well-documented, but the specific events of individual years are difficult to date precisely.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 871 BCE is sparse and almost entirely dependent on Assyrian royal inscriptions. The perspective is heavily skewed toward Assyrian imperial success. Local perspectives are largely absent.

graph_edges.note: Continuation of the same campaign cycle
